title: SPOT-OD v1.3.1 validation-selected edge-only residual-refinement ablation
description: SPOT-OD v1.3.1-validation-selected-residual-refine adds a validation-selected edge-only retained-candidate residual-refinement ablation. The --node-disagreement-features omit setting removes eight node disagreement aggregate fields, reducing node feature dimension from 30 to 22 while keeping edge feature dimension at 10. The model uses development validation seed minimum 53, 82 fit/training samples, 37 validation samples, and validation_loss checkpoint selection. In the edge-only attention residual-refine configuration, all non-development rows reach 390.317 m versus the best retained candidate at 459.591 m, a 15.073% gain; fresh source-generation seeds 151/157/163/167 reach 386.373 m versus 445.943 m, a 13.358% gain. Versus the matched edge-only local/no-message control, attention has 24.835% all-non-development and 45.329% fresh advantages. Versus the edge-only mean graph, attention has smaller 3.983% all-non-development and 5.548% fresh advantages. Evidence scope remains limited to retained-candidate compact-simulator evidence; graph-path isolation is only against the matched edge-only no-message local control. This release is not operational POD, not public precise-reference validation, not independent-machine reproduction, not a full raw/training/all-filter rerun, not standalone learned recursive filtering, not broad learned-OD validation, and not operational learned OD.
notes: This v1.3.1 release is not metadata-only. It adds the validation-selected edge-only retained-candidate residual-refinement ablation for tag v1.3.1-validation-selected-residual-refine at commit c4882f1b367426c0966e906b9332f64d44d2279f. The GitHub release is https://github.com/Alavi1412/spot-od-reproduction/releases/tag/v1.3.1-validation-selected-residual-refine with asset spot_od_v1_3_1_validation_selected_residual_refine.zip. Because this metadata file is included inside that asset, the corrective replacement asset byte size and SHA-256 are reported externally after archive construction rather than embedded here. The Zenodo record is https://zenodo.org/records/20844596, version DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20844596, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20768672, with archived source file Alavi1412/spot-od-reproduction-v1.3.1-validation-selected-residual-refine.zip (212,947,668 bytes; MD5 863e5077d4d29a827c6fcfd1181dce34). The --node-disagreement-features omit setting removes eight node disagreement aggregate fields, reducing node dimension from 30 to 22 while retaining edge dimension 10. Development model selection uses validation seed minimum 53, 82 fit/training samples, 37 validation samples, and validation_loss checkpoint selection. Edge-only attention residual-refine reaches 390.317 m across all non-development rows versus the best retained candidate at 459.591 m (15.073% gain) and versus the matched edge-only local/no-message control at 519.282 m (24.835% advantage). Fresh seeds 151/157/163/167 reach 386.373 m versus the best retained candidate at 445.943 m (13.358% gain) and versus the matched local control at 706.730 m (45.329% advantage). The edge-only mean graph is closer, with attention-vs-mean advantages of 3.983% all non-development and 5.548% fresh; the future-only attention-vs-mean check remains weak/mixed. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20842573 is cited as the prior v1.3.0 version. Scope limits: retained-candidate compact-simulator evidence only; graph-path isolation only against the matched edge-only no-message local control; not operational POD, not public precise-reference validation, not independent-machine reproduction, not full raw/training/all-filter rerun, not standalone learned recursive filtering, not broad learned-OD validation, and not operational learned OD.
